concepts.category: One of the Most Important Facts
concepts.title: Malware Types
concepts.description: There are two types of malware: Viruses and Trojans. Viruses can replicate themselves and spread to other systems, while Trojans are disguised as legitimate software that can allow hackers to access a system.

concepts.title: Cyber Laws
concepts.description: India has several cyber laws, including the Information Technology Act (IT Act) of 2000, which deals with cyber crimes and provides penalties for various offenses.

concepts.title: Types of Phishing Attacks
concepts.description: Phishing attacks can be categorized into two main types: Spear Phishing and Whaling. Spear Phishing targets specific individuals or organizations, while Whaling targets high-level executives or decision-makers.

concepts.title: Password Security
concepts.description: Use the 'Password Rule of Thumb' mnemonic: 'P@ssw0rd must be at least 12 characters long, contain uppercase and lowercase letters, numbers, and special characters.'

concepts.title: Weak Passwords
concepts.description: Avoid using easily guessable information, such as your name, birthdate, or common words. Also, do not reuse passwords across multiple accounts.

concepts.title: Types of Firewalls
concepts.description: A firewall can be categorized into three types: Network-based, Host-based, and Application-based. Network-based firewalls are used at the network level, host-based firewalls are used at the device level, and application-based firewalls are used at the application level.

concepts.title: Data Backup
concepts.description: Regularly back up your important data to an external drive or cloud storage to prevent data loss in case of a cyber attack or system failure.

concepts.title: Types of Cyber Attacks
concepts.description: Cyber attacks can be categorized into three main types: Network-based attacks, Application-based attacks, and Social Engineering attacks. Network-based attacks target network vulnerabilities, application-based attacks target software vulnerabilities, and social engineering attacks target human psychology.
